headline: AI dominates March job cuts while Florida metros face disproportionate risk—but South Florida tech sector keeps hiring

stories.title: AI Cited in 25% of March Job Cuts as Displacement Accelerates
stories.summary: AI-related job cuts jumped to 15,341 positions in March (25% of all layoffs), up from just 5% in 2025. Orlando & Tampa ranked among the most vulnerable metros, with nearly 1 in 3 workers without degrees exposed to automation in gateway jobs.
stories.takeaway: The acceleration is real—AI went from a minor factor to the #1 reason for layoffs in just months, fr.

stories.title: Florida Projected to Gain 14,453 Tech Jobs in 2026 Despite AI Anxiety
stories.summary: CompTIA's latest report shows Florida expecting the 3rd most tech job growth nationwide. AI fluency is now required by 275k+ job postings, but soft skills like communication & problem-solving are still being overlooked.
stories.takeaway: Tech opportunities are lowkey expanding in FL even as everyone panics about AI—but companies want people who can actually communicate too.

stories.title: South Florida Tech Market Still Strong—Median Salary Up 18.5% Since Early 2024
stories.summary: Over 50k unique tech jobs posted across Miami-Dade, Broward & Palm Beach since January 2024. AI skills grew 266% but traditional CS still dominates. Companies filling roles in just 22 days avg.
stories.takeaway: Tbh South Florida is quietly building serious tech momentum—salaries are rising faster than inflation & employers are actually hungry for talent.

stories.title: Tech Hiring Surges Back Despite AI Replacement Fears—67k+ Openings Now
stories.summary: Software engineering job postings exceeded 67k as of April 4, more than double the mid-2023 low. Recruitment analytics firm TrueUp says the AI-will-replace-engineers narrative doesn't match actual hiring demand.
stories.takeaway: Engineering roles aren't getting automated away yet—companies building AI still need skilled people to actually build it.

watchNext.title: Entry-Level Workers Face Sharpest AI Exposure—89% of 2026 Grads Worried
watchNext.why: Monster's State of the Graduate Report shows anxiety about AI displacement jumped 25 points in 12 months. Young people shifting priorities toward job security over career growth.

watchNext.title: AI Reshaping Not Erasing White-Collar Work—Experience Still Wins
watchNext.why: Harvard Business Review research shows generative AI is augmenting rather than replacing experienced workers. Tacit knowledge & judgment become more valuable, not less.
